title: lobelia laxiflora var. stricta
intro: Lobelia laxiflora var. stricta, also known as Kamahi or Bush Lobelia, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and is characterized by its tall, upright stems and blue or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it useful for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Lobelia laxiflora var. stricta include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs, and ease congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Lobelia laxiflora var. stricta is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
